name: Rust (Tokio + Axum)
systemContext: Rust project using Tokio for async runtime. Axum for HTTP if applicable. Follows clean architecture: domain, application, infrastructure layers.

rules: Never use .unwrap() or .expect() in production code — use ? operator
All public functions that can fail return Result<T, E>
Domain layer: pure Rust, no framework dependencies
Application layer: orchestrates domain, owns use cases
Infrastructure layer: DB, HTTP, external services
Dependencies point inward — infrastructure depends on domain, never reverse
Prefer async/await over manual Future implementations
Use Arc<T> for shared state across tasks
Avoid blocking calls in async context — use spawn_blocking
Run cargo fmt before every commit
All clippy warnings must be resolved
Public API must have doc comments (///)
Module names: snake_case, types: PascalCase, constants: SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE

hooks.command: cd {projectDir} && cargo fmt && cargo clippy -- -D warnings 2>&1 | head -30

forbiddenZones.reason: Lock file changes require dependency audit
